channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Surprise: Basing College Admissions On Merit Instead Of Skin Is Good For Everyone
item.description: New evidence shows that the Supreme Court’s ban on affirmative action is not a setback; instead, it facilitates improved outcomes for all.

item.title: California Democrats Pile Out Of Clown Car At Their Convention
item.description: An all-you-can-eat buffet of free stuff and Trump sucks represented the ideological substance of California's late-AWFL Khmer Rouge party.

item.title: Trump impeachment: The issue Democrats can't quite pick up -- or put down
item.description: Rep. Jasmine Crockett isn't shy about it -- she wants to impeach President Trump.

item.title: How ‘dumb money’ took over stock markets: $5.4 trillion of retail activity took place in 2025
item.description: All told, money coming into the market from individual investors jumped about 50% from 2023 to early 2025, JPMorgan found.

item.title: Northeast Corridor gathers snow at 3 inches an hour as National Weather Service calls traffic ‘nearly impossible’
item.description: Blizzard warnings stretched from Maryland to Maine, while over 5,000 flights were canceled.

item.title: Federal judge blocks release of Jack Smith report's second volume
item.description: The judge’s ruling blocks the release of Jack Smith’s report on President Donald Trump’s handling of classified documents. The report was slated for p...

item.title: Family of Iowa grad killed by illegal immigrant to attend Trump's State of the Union
item.description: Sarah Root’s family will attend President Trump’s State of the Union after his administration extradited the illegal immigrant convicted in her 2016 d...

item.title: Is the Epstein Files Frenzy Turning into Trial by PDF?
item.description: LA Mayor Karen Bass’s call for Casey Wasserman to resign from the LA28 Olympic Committee reflects only panic and moral posturing.

item.title: State Dept orders evacuation of non-emergency US personnel from embassy in Beirut
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration ordered non-essential personnel to evacuate the U.S. Embassy in Beirut, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Fortune 500’s DEI Retreat Shows The Corporate Social Credit System Is Collapsing
item.description: Fortune 500 companies are waking up and understanding what time it is. It’s time for businesses to get back to business.

item.title: Democrats name candidates to 'Red to Blue' initiative, aiming to flip GOP majority during midterms
item.description: The DCCC announced candidates it is backing as part of its “Red to Blue" initiative as they seek to clinch the House majority during the midterm elect...

item.title: Conservatives, GOP lawmakers slam HuffPost critique on displays of patriotism at Olympics
item.description: Republican lawmakers and conservatives on social media are blasting a HuffPost article suggesting that it's understandable that Americans feel less pa...

item.title: Colorado Bill Would Take Kids Away From Parents Who Refuse To Trans Them
item.description: Colorado legislators support requiring courts to consider whether parents embrace a child’s 'gender identity' in determining custody.

item.title: State Department Works To Revoke Visa Of U.K. Operative Who Allegedly Censored The Federalist, Other Americans
item.description: The State Department announced its intention to revoke the visas of five foreign individuals who have allegedly censored Americans.

item.title: U.S. trade partners delay meetings, demand clarity after the Supreme Court guts Trump's tariff power
item.description: Nations that negotiated trade deals with President Trump are demanding clarity from the U.S. and scrutinizing their options after the Supreme Court st...

item.title: Mexico’s latest cartel violence prompts fears of ‘narcoterrorism’ in replay 1990s Colombia
item.description: The Mexican army killed the country’s most powerful cartel leader and one of the United States’ most wanted fugitives on Sunday, notching a major vict...

item.title: States Should Put Workers First with Private Sector Labor Reforms
item.description: It’s no accident that right-to-work states have the highest net domestic population growth.

item.title: Newsom ripped over 'racist' viral clip telling Black mayor 'I'm like you' before touting poor SAT score
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom is facing heat after telling a Black mayor he is "like you" before describing his poor SAT score and trouble reading spee...

item.title: Judge blocks release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump classified documents case
item.description: A federal judge on Monday permanently barred the release of a report by special counsel Jack Smith on his investigation into President Trump's hoardin...

item.title: Blockbuster ratings start to vindicate NBC’s $7.75 billion bet on the Olympics
item.description: NBC averaged 24 million viewers across its prime afternoon coverage and Primetime in Milan, a 94% improvement over the 2022 Beijing Games.

item.title: Manufacturers call for clear, consistent trade framework
item.description: The National Association of Manufacturers wants a new trade framework from Congress after the Supreme Court nixed President Trump's major tariffs.

item.title: Self-driving taxis hit London, a city with such complex streets that it has a ‘Knowledge’ test that takes cabbies years to pass
item.description: Self-driving taxis are “a solution looking for a problem,” said Steven McNamara, general secretary of the Licensed Taxi Drivers' Association.

item.title: Building Commitment to Long-Term Investing
item.description: Why clients stall on long-term investing and how reframing commitment around flexibility unlocks forward momentum. The post Building Commitment to Lon...

item.title: Populism Is Having a Moment in Australia
item.description: Will it last? Only if the country’s One Nation party can avoid the pitfalls experienced by other populist parties.

item.title: Don’t Prosecute Women for Abortion
item.description: While defending unborn children is essential, criminalizing women who have abortions is no way to show compassion.

item.title: How Trump’s ‘Prior Authorization’ Deal Puts Patients Ahead of Paperwork
item.description: Speeding up insurers’ authorization process will deliver care more quickly and cut down on unnecessary red tape.

item.title: Why Static Portfolios Fail When Risk Regimes Change
item.description: Why static portfolio frameworks fail when risk regimes shift, drawing lessons from the very different market breakdowns of 2020 and 2022. The post Why...

item.title: What the Market Knows That WACC Doesn’t
item.description: Market-implied discount rates reveal how investors price risk, often diverging from WACC and reshaping capital decisions. The post What the Market Kno...

item.title: Attention Bias in AI-Driven Investing
item.description: AI tools may favor popular stocks over overlooked ones, embedding attention bias into investment decisions. The post Attention Bias in AI-Driven Inves...

item.title: Social Security Claiming Strategies for High-Net-Worth Clients
item.description: Social Security claiming is a capital-allocation decision for affluent clients. This analysis weighs taxes, longevity risk, and liquidity trade-offs. ...
